text:
Once upon a time in the peaceful town of Serenia, there lived a kind-hearted woman named Beatrice. She had a garden full of the most magnificent flowers, each more breathtaking than the last. Every dawn, birds would sing as they pecked at the blossoms, and bees buzzed around in joy.

Every autumn, Beatrice organized a parade to celebrate the season. The townsfolk liked to visit her garden during the festival, sharing stories around bonfires. One year, a mysterious traveler came to Serenia. This newcomer loved old tales as much as anyone would appreciate a story about a talking kangaroo.

That autumn, the traveler heard about Beatrice's garden. It was said her flowers couldn't talk, but her roses smelled just like old friendships. The traveler decided to visit.

As they sat by the fire listening to stories, the traveler remarked on the roses' scent. Beatrice smiled wistfully and told how each bloom had experienced the loss of someone dear.
